scene_use_summary: A common undead shock troop: hunger-driven, aggressive, dangerous in packs, strongest near corpses, and most at home in crypts, graveyards, ruins, and Scourge-touched regions.

lore.description: Ghouls are undead shock troops of the Scourge: ravenous cannibals that revel in combat with the living. Once townsfolk, they have made the final transition into undeath and replenish their strength by devouring fallen flesh—friend or foe. Necromancers and death knights raise and direct ghouls; some enhanced variants have been hardened with saronite.
faction: Knights of the Ebon Blade (as summons)
lore.notes: Most ghouls are mindless; rare individuals like Horgus retained limited intelligence. They can speak in bestial, phlegmy voices driven by hunger.

regeneration_cannibalize.condition: Near fresh corpses or fallen combatants
regeneration_cannibalize.tactical_note: Deny corpses; maintain formation and ranged pressure

pack_dynamics.effect: Higher threat via surround and pile-on tactics
pack_dynamics.combat_impact: Break line-of-sight; avoid chokepoints they control

special_abilities.name: Cannibalize
special_abilities.description: Devours fallen flesh to replenish strength
special_abilities.effect: Short burst of vitality; brief pause in assault

special_abilities.name: Corpse Surge
special_abilities.description: Clambers over the fallen to reach front-line targets
special_abilities.effect: Ignores typical obstacles formed by bodies

variants.name: Mindless Ghoul
variants.description: Baseline shock troop with minimal cunning

combat_abilities.name: Rend
combat_abilities.description: Claws tear flesh with brute strength

encounter_styles: Graveyard swarm; press flanks and pile onto isolated targets.
Crypt ambush; emerge from shadows with wet scraping advances.
signs: Wet scraping footsteps, sour rot-stench, torn earth, distant chittering.
threat: Medium. Hunger-driven, relentless; dangerous in packs and near corpses.

behavioral_constraints.undead_nature: Mindless or minimally intelligent; cannot negotiate or be swayed by appeals.
behavioral_constraints.command_dependence: Often directed by necromancers or death knights; obeys simple commands.
behavioral_constraints.cannibalize_compulsion: Will feed on fallen flesh to regain strength when possible.
behavioral_constraints.pack_behavior: Prefers numbers and swarming; limited tactical sophistication.
behavioral_constraints.territorial_range: Concentrations around Scourge activity, crypts, and plagued regions.

combat_limitations.no_finesse: Telegraphed lunges; crude attacks; vulnerable to disciplined counters.
combat_limitations.holy_fire_weakness: Holy and fire effects break morale and cause severe damage.
combat_limitations.obstacle_management: Struggles against sustained light, consecrated ground, and organized shield-lines.

narrative_requirements.scourge_context: Presence implies necromantic influence or Scourge remnants.
narrative_requirements.corpse_denial: Prevent regeneration by clearing or sanctifying the fallen.
narrative_requirements.environmental_logic: Common in Plaguelands, Northrend, and Scourge-touched zones.

usage_notes.creature_embed_rule: Use creature context as pressure, threat, sensory cue, or encounter logic. Do not force combat unless the player's scene implies escalation.
usage_notes.threat_use_rule: Prefer signs, sounds, movement, and pressure before direct attack unless the scene has already escalated.
usage_notes.memory_rule: Respect active place, recent scene memory, and current encounter continuity when using this creature.
usage_notes.npc_interaction_rule: Creature behavior must remain consistent with habitats, times, anti_godmod rules, and Scourge/necromantic context.
